concept: Hungry / The Hollow Asking / In Need of Food
description: Hungry: the belly's emptiness announcing itself. With 'nuora' (food) and 'nuola' (eat) it completes eating's little grammar: state, object, act. Also, quietly, one of the most important words a peace language can have — most conflict history is hunger history.
sound_symbolism: Hollow 'ku', grounded 'mo', and 'li' the thin asking sound — emptiness that speaks up.
grammatical_notes: Predicative: 'mia kumoli nai.' (I am hungry.) The host's question: 'wa thia kumoli nai.' (Are you hungry?) With the answer: 'no nuola.' (Eat.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Feeding the hungry begins with hearing them; the plain adjective is the first sentence of every food-sharing culture.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The year was planned around the hungry months; the word carried the calendar's most serious information.
